Muster.topic: list of topics to differentiate between musical and non-musical content
Muster.sector: sparte code (e.g. sparte004) indicates sector-specific mapping; null = any sector
class: E: '' (deferred — aco:AudioExpression for Group B when in scope)

default.remarks: Fallback for any mt001 record not matched by a specific dc:type. Excludes Foto, Archivalie (handled by other dispatch layers).

Audiofile.remarks: Often recordings of theater performances. Digital audio file; carrier format for non-musical authored audio.

Band.remarks: Often an audio book. Publication unit; audio book content is authored non-musical speech.

CD.remarks: Compact disc audio carrier. All CD records in this corpus are musical.

Folie.remarks: Phonographic foil. Usual topic: Lied or Song. Musical flexible-disc carrier format.

Hörfunksendung.remarks: Radio broadcast. Authored non-musical audio document at Manifestation level.

Inszenierung.remarks: Audio recording of a stage production. Often recordings of theater performances. Non-musical authored work.

Interview.remarks: Recorded interview. Speech content; no musical content. Generic audio carrier.

Kardiologie.remarks: Topic used as dc:type in some Magnettonband records. Treated as generic audio carrier at Manifestation level. Sector: sparte006.

Langspielplatte.remarks: Long-play vinyl disc. Usual topic: Oper or Opera. Musical carrier format for extended classical recordings.

Magnettonband.remarks: Magnetic tape reel. Topics: Mono, Stereo (technical characteristics). Used in research and lecture recording contexts; content is non-musical. Sector: sparte004.

Magnettonband / Vortrag.remarks: Magnetic tape recording of a lecture (Vortrag). Compound dc:type explicitly names carrier (tape) and content (lecture). Non-musical authored speech. Sector: sparte006.

Mono.remarks: Monophonic audio recording. Technical carrier descriptor in the Research sector context. Non-musical. Sector: sparte004.

Monografie.remarks: Monograph in audio form (audio book or spoken-word recording of a scholarly work). Publication type, not a musical form.

Platte.remarks: Disc. In the Research sector, audio discs are catalogued as research audio documents. Distinguished from Schallplatte (explicitly musical) by generic term and sector. Sector: sparte004.

Schallfolie.remarks: Thin flexible phonographic disc. Topics: Lied, Schlager, Kirchenmusik, Oper, Arie, Tonträger — all musical. Treated as unambiguously musical across all corpus instances.

Schallplatte.remarks: Standard vinyl phonograph record. Canonical German library term for a musical disc carrier.

Schellackplatte.remarks: Shellac disc (78 rpm). Audio carrier exclusively associated with early musical recordings.

Sprechplatte.remarks: Speech disc for spoken-word recordings (poetry, radio plays, speeches). Explicitly non-musical; handles Schallfolie records whose topic is not in the musical topic list.

Stereo.remarks: Stereophonic audio recording. Technical carrier descriptor in the Research sector context. Non-musical. Sector: sparte004.

Ton.remarks: Sound/audio recording in the Museum sector. Museum audio objects in this corpus are predominantly musical. Sector: sparte006.

Tonaufnahme.remarks: Audio recording. Topics: Tierstimme, Industriegeräusche, Lesung, Interview. Generic carrier label; content varies widely. Sector: sparte002.

Tondokument.remarks: Audio document. Topic: Musicethnologie (ethnomusicological field recordings). Musical class assigned on basis of corpus content. Open question: if other corpora contain non-ethnomusicological Tondokument records, this assignment is wrong — topic-gating not currently supported by the CSV lookup model.

Tondokumente.remarks: Plural of Tondokument; 3 instances of city council meeting recordings. Deliberative speech, not music.
